name: Special Characters in Biomedical Labels
input: Create a flowchart showing a drug interaction pathway. Start with 'CYP3A4/CYP2D6 Metabolism'. Branch to 'P-glycoprotein (P-gp) Efflux' and 'Phase II Conjugation (UGT1A1 & UGT2B7)'. P-gp leads to 'Blood-Brain Barrier (BBB) Transport'. Phase II leads to 'Glucuronidation → Sulfation → Acetylation'. Both converge at 'Renal Excretion (GFR > 90 mL/min)'. Add a side branch from CYP3A4 to 'Drug-Drug Interaction Risk (↑ with ketoconazole, ↓ with rifampin)'. Include IC50 = 2.5 μM annotation.
qualityCriteria: Special characters preserved: slashes (CYP3A4/CYP2D6), parentheses, ampersands (&), arrows (→), Greek letters (μ)
All 7 nodes present with correct labels
Branch from CYP3A4 shows two parallel paths
Convergence at Renal Excretion is clear
No syntax errors from special characters
Valid Mermaid syntax that parses without errors
napkinBenchmark: Flowchart with properly escaped special characters, clean branching, no Mermaid parse errors from slashes, ampersands, or Unicode symbols.
